Agus Rucula.heroHook: Argentine urban artist Agus Rucula (@agus.rucula): Murales Buenos Aires (2018) cites murals since 2012; VenUS Urban Art lists festivals across the Americas and Europe.
Agus Rucula.storyFullText:
Interview-based profiles identify Agus Rucula as an Argentine urban artist and teacher who centers the human body, gesture, and community dialogue in large-scale work.

Murales Buenos Aires (2018) notes she has painted murals since 2012 and discusses process links to dance and photography.

Italian press (e.g., Giornale di Brescia) documents recent murals in Italy; VenUS Urban Art summarizes international festival participation.

Author databases (Amauta) list design studies at UBA—verify current city and spelling of accented surname on official channels.
Agus Rucula.pressText:
Murales Buenos Aires — 2018 — Interview (ES) — https://muralesbuenosaires.com.ar/2018/12/11/entrevista-a-agus-rucula/
Giornale di Brescia — — Murals in Brescia — https://www.giornaledibrescia.it/cultura/arte/due-nuovi-murales-in-via-morosini-sono-di-agus-rucula-e-nsn997-k1zbhf7m
Agus Rucula.notes: Some directories use @agusrucula without dot; Street Collector CSV uses @agus.rucula.

Max Diamond.heroHook: Max Diamond (@maxdiamond52): illustrator sharing poster and fan-art work on Instagram; public posts include Severance for Apple TV—verify commercial credits separately.
Max Diamond.storyFullText:
Instagram posts indexed in search show Max Diamond publishing illustrated posters, including work referencing the Apple TV series Severance.

Linktree aggregates social profiles; treat follower counts and self-descriptions as self-reported.

No single authoritative biography URL was identified in the search pass; prefer future interviews for education and location.

Confirm licensing before using entertainment IP-derived fan art in brand marketing.

Ezra Baderman.heroHook: British-Israeli artist Ezra Baderman (@ezrabaderman): ezrabaderman.com states Paris birth, London upbringing, Lisbon home; Bezalel training and mixed-media practice.
Ezra Baderman.storyFullText:
ezrabaderman.com/about states he is British-Israeli, Paris-born and London-raised, now living in Lisbon, with studies at John Cass Foundation and Bezalel.

The same site describes collage, painting, UV installations, and a Tel Aviv street-poster salvage series with peace-oriented messaging.

Saatchi Art hosts a selling profile under his name—use for medium lists, not for uncredited quotes.

Verify Hebrew/English marketing claims with the artist before publication.

Marylou Faure.activeSince: Freelance illustration ~2015–2016 per It’s Nice That (2016)
Marylou Faure.heroHook: French illustrator Marylou Faure (@maryloufaure): Penninghen-trained, London-based per It’s Nice That (2016); Coca-Cola, McLaren F1, YouTube in later profiles.
Marylou Faure.storyFullText:
It’s Nice That (2016) profiles Marylou Faure’s move from Paris graphic training (Penninghen) into London agency work and freelance illustration.

maryloufaure.com/pages/about lists solo exhibitions across London, Paris, Dublin, Beijing, and Sofia, plus talks at Adobe, Apple, and Pictoplasma.

Design Week (2020) discusses her strong, eyeless female characters and commercial roster including McLaren F1 and YouTube.

Creative Boom and other design outlets repeat client names—confirm live roster on her site before ads.
Marylou Faure.pressText:
It’s Nice That — 2016 — Profile — http://www.itsnicethat.com/articles/mary-lou-faure-illustration-080216
Design Week — 2020 — Interview — https://www.designweek.co.uk/issues/14-20-september-2020/marylou-faure-profile/

Dima Korma.heroHook: Berlin abstract muralist Dima Korma (@dimakorma): Book a Street Artist cites large-format walls; site kormadima.com lists 2025 Tampa and Barcelona mural projects.
Dima Korma.storyFullText:
Book a Street Artist describes Dima Korma as a Berlin-based abstract muralist combining textures and typography for interiors and exteriors.

kormadima.com project pages (as indexed) reference large corporate murals in Tampa (2025) and a Barcelona court mural with Rebobinart (2025).

Walls of Hope documents a commemorative mural related to Erez Kalderon—verify context from the foundation’s materials.

Threads and Linktree mirror the same handle @dimakorma.

Samme Snow.heroHook: London illustrator Samme Snow (@snowhandsworld): sammesnow.com cites Farnham graphic degree (2013); Canary Wharf, House of Vans, GQ Middle East commissions.
Samme Snow.storyFullText:
sammesnow.com/about states Samme Snow completed a Graphic Communication degree at the University for the Creative Arts, Farnham, in 2013.

The same page lists murals and brand work including Canary Wharf, House of Vans, and GQ Middle East, plus community colouring workshops since 2020.

Portfolio pages show hospitality and workplace murals across London and other UK cities.

Street Collector product pages reference his lamp edition context.

Sancho.heroHook: Sancho (@bysancho): sanchosancho.com traces Birmingham/Shanghai street influence; Amsterdam base per Wallbaby; NBCUniversal, Revolut named as collaborators.
Sancho.storyFullText:
sanchosancho.com/about describes a visual artist and illustrator blending hand-drawn character design with pop culture and humor.

The biography references creative roots in Birmingham and Shanghai before current practice; Wallbaby lists Amsterdam as a base for print sales.

The about page names collaborations with NBCUniversal, LG, Wrike, Revolut, and Carlsberg Group.

Awards listed include Saatchi Art selections and Fine Acts grants—verify dates on the live page.

Alice Bureau.heroHook: Zurich illustrator Alice Hoffmann as Bureau Alice (@bureau_alice): hueandeye.org notes 20+ years as art director before illustration; UBS, Adobe, Meta named.
Alice Bureau.storyFullText:
Hue & Eye profiles explain that Alice Hoffmann worked over two decades as an art/creative director in European agencies before launching Bureau Alice.

bureaualice.com and illustratoren-schweiz.ch list packaging, editorial, and mural commissions for UBS, Adobe, Meta, Diptyque, Deutsche Bahn, and others.

Her process notes describe analog drawing refined in Adobe Illustrator.

Behance uses the handle alicealice for some archives—confirm cross-links.
Alice Bureau.pressText: Hue & Eye — — Bureau Alice feature — https://www.hueandeye.org/bureau-alice/

Alin Mor.activeSince: Freelance illustrator/graphic designer since 2016 (LinkedIn); Bezalel B.Des 2014–2017
Alin Mor.heroHook: Haifa illustrator Alin Mor (@alinmor): alinmor.com lists Bezalel B.Des (2014–2017); themes include femininity and spirituality; Meta Israel cited among collaborators.
Alin Mor.storyFullText:
alinmor.com/about outlines a Haifa-based practice exploring femininity, archetypes, spirituality, and large painted walls for public and private clients.

The same page lists exhibitions at Illustration Week, Shaar 3 Gallery, Jaffa Museum, and press mentions in Elephant and Haaretz.

Collaborators named include Meta Israel, Suzuki Israel, and She Shreds Magazine.

LinkedIn aligns on Bezalel degree dates and freelance start year.

Beto Val.activeSince: Public practice accelerated during pandemic per elbetoval.com blog essays
Beto Val.heroHook: Ecuadorian collage artist Beto Val (@elbetoval): elbetoval.com blogs trace pandemic-era practice; NYT Kids Edition cover mentioned in third-party profiles—confirm on primary.
Beto Val.storyFullText:
elbetoval.com blog posts describe surreal digital collage built from vintage scientific illustration sources.

Third-party profiles (Colossal, Rubin Museum mentions in commerce copy) should be double-checked against primary press links before reuse.

iCanvas and Street Collector list editions—good for SKU context, not for biography.

Threads mirrors @elbetoval for updates.
Beto Val.notes: Verify NYT Kids credit on original publisher page before claiming in ads.

Cokorda Martin.activeSince: Career start 2013; born Klungkung 1995 (Timeout interview, Ana-tomy journal)
Cokorda Martin.heroHook: Balinese illustrator Cokorda Martin (@treasurecult.work): Timeout Asia (2026) interview; born Klungkung 1995; Uniqlo Indonesia, Café Kitsuné collaborations noted.
Cokorda Martin.storyFullText:
Timeout’s 2026 feature interviews Cokorda Martin about Balinese heritage, color, and global brand work.

Ana-tomy’s journal post Celebrating Individuality summarizes his family art lineage and fusion of traditional motifs with pop palettes.

linktr.ee/cokordamartin points to representation (Debut Art) and shops.

Instagram posts include music-album homage series—credit original musicians in marketing.

Aviv Shamir.heroHook: Illustrator Aviv Shamir (@avivos_91): listed on Street Collector internal research sheet; no third-party biography URL verified in the public search pass.
Aviv Shamir.storyFullText:
The Street Collector artist-research CSV lists Instagram @avivos_91 without an accompanying biography URL.

Automated web search under the exact name did not return a definitive portfolio or interview matching the handle.

Before publishing marketing copy, load the live Instagram profile and any site linked in bio.

Leave exhibition and press lines empty until primary sources are captured.
Aviv Shamir.notes: Mostly empty fields until primary bio URL is confirmed.

Hen Macabi.heroHook: Israeli illustrator Hen Macabi (@henmacabi): Haaretz (2019) reports his graphic work on Netta Barzilai single art; henmacabi.com hosts music-design portfolio.
Hen Macabi.storyFullText:
Haaretz published reporting in 2019 describing Hen Macabi as the graphic artist behind artwork for Netta Barzilai’s single Bassa Sababa.

henmacabi.com catalogs album and poster commissions for Israeli musicians across multiple years.

Street Collector / Street Lamp product pages reference his typography-forward lamp edition.

Confirm any additional celebrity credits against the artist’s own discography lists.
Hen Macabi.pressText: Haaretz — 2019 — Reporting on Eurovision-era single artwork — https://www.haaretz.com/israel-news/eurovision/2019-01-31/ty-article-magazine/.premium/hen-macabi-the-graphic-artist-who-designed-the-cover-for-netta-barzilais-new-singl/0000017f-dbdb-df62-a9ff-dfdf79a40000

Refiloe Mnisi.heroHook: Refiloe Mnisi (@urfavsweatpants): Street Collector research sheet lists this handle; no matching biography URL surfaced in the automated search pass.
Refiloe Mnisi.storyFullText:
Internal CSV ties the display name Refiloe Mnisi to @urfavsweatpants without an external portfolio link.

Search results for the handle plus surname did not return a reliable artist biography during the research pass.

Confirm country, medium, and pronouns directly with the artist before writing long-form story text.

Do not infer identity from unrelated South African news profiles with similar first names.

Geometric Bang.activeSince: Graffiti since late 1990s per Varsi / Wood’d profiles
Geometric Bang.heroHook: Mattia Botta aka Geometric Bang (@geometricbang): Varsi Lab bio cites Lodi-born (1984), Florence-based; murals in Rome, Genoa, Tokyo and international group shows.
Geometric Bang.storyFullText:
Varsi Art & Lab and Wood’d blog posts identify Geometric Bang as Mattia Botta, born in Lodi in 1984 and active in Florence, moving from graffiti toward illustration-heavy walls.

Organiconcrete (2014) and BOOOOOOOM (2013) provide early interviews about pencils, spray paint, and exhibitions.

Press lists mural travel across Europe, Asia, and Africa—verify each city claim against primary photo documentation.

He also works on canvas and, per later interviews, tattooing.

Laura Fridman.heroHook: French artist Laura Fridman (@laura_fridman_art): laurafridman.art/about cites Yale economics then Israel Ballet; Tel Aviv base; 2022 Kuli Alma solo listed.
Laura Fridman.storyFullText:
laurafridman.art/about states she is French, studied economics at Yale, danced professionally with Israel Ballet, and now paints full-time.

The same page lists solo and group shows including Kuli Alma, Tel Aviv (2022) and international cities such as Barcelona and Lisbon.

Her figurative style exaggerates hands and necks while keeping faces realistic—a motif described on Street Collector lamp copy.

Verify future fair dates (e.g., Turin) on the live site before publishing.
